data.title: Diageo evaluating options for China assets - Bloomberg
data.description:
Diageo (DEO) is considering options for its Chinese assets, including potential sales. 

Diageo (DEO) is working with Goldman Sachs and UBS Group AG to look at its operations, which include a more than 63% stake in Shanghai-listed Sichuan Swellfun Co., according to a  Bloomberg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The advisers are seeking initial interest from local strategic buyers and private equity firms, according to the report. 

Considerations are preliminary, and no final decisions have been made, Bloomberg reported.

Diageo, Goldman Sachs, and UBS declined to comment to Bloomberg. A representative for Sichuan Swellfun said the company hasn’t received any information about a stake sale.

Diageo ADRs fell 1%.

data.title: Pimco expects Fannie-Freddie MBS purchases to lower mortgage rates - report
data.description:
Last week, President Donald Trump's ordered U.S. mortgage giants Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) to purchase $200B in mortgage-backed securities, in a move to lower mortgage rates to make homeownership more affordable.

Pacific Investment Management Co. Chief Investment Officer Daniel Ivascyn thinks the plan could help.

"We definitely think it could bring down rates and reduce the spread on mortgage-backed securities," he told Bloomberg News in an interview. The announcement provides "another tailwind" for Pimco's overweight holdings in the debt, which it has held through 2025, he added.

Trump's announcement “wasn’t the driver behind our ‘overweight’ decision, but it was always one potential catalyst for more aggressive spread tightening,” Ivascyn said.

In response to the administration's imminent purchase of MBSs, the Bloomberg MBS index gained 0.53% on Friday, its best single-day increase since the beginning of August, Bloomberg said.

Still, he points to the supply-demand mismatch at the root of the housing market affordability issue. "The long-term problem is that we have too few homes versus the number of households, and the only long-term solution will be to increase the housing stock," he said.

If spreads between MBSs and Treasuries tighten further, Pimco may reduce its overweight MBS holdings in the $217B Pimco income fund  "a bit" over time, Ivascyn said.

data.title: U.S. stocks slip as retail inflation comes in slightly cooler, earnings fail to lift sentiment
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were lower on Tuesday as investors assessed core CPI inflation, which came in slightly cooler than consensus, and some important earnings reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.3% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.7%.

The December Consumer Price Index came in at +0.3% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.7% YoY vs. +2.6% consensus and +2.7% prior.

Core CPI (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, +2.6% YoY vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior.

“Tuesday's CPI was in-line with expectations and that will likely keep the Federal Reserve on pause when it comes to interest rates for the foreseeable future… Recent positive employment data, elevated inflation, sticky price levels and political noise will keep the Fed at bay through at least the Spring,” said Skyler Weinand, CIO at Regan Capital.

“CPI is still under a cloud from the missing October number. Inflation is still relatively high, but trending downward. Consumers are still grappling with high overall price levels and sticker shock from the post-Covid jarring in housing, healthcare, education and everyday purchase prices,” Weinand added.

Also, new home sales slipped 0.1% MoM to 737K in October, vs. 716K expected, from 738K in the prior month, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

Among companies reporting on Tuesday, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) -3% posted better-than-expected earnings, while Delta Air Lines (DAL) -1.5% had year-over-year revenue growth. 

Big companies reporting this week include Bank of America (BAC), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Citigroup (C).

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was largely flat at 4.18%, while the U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was also unchanged at 3.54%. Investors are awaiting a 30-Year bond auction later this afternoon.

data.title: Oppenheimer downgrades Adobe to Perform as demotions pile up
data.description:
Oppenheimer is the latest financial firm to downgrade Adobe, rerating the creative software company to Perform from Outperform.

"We had previously been bullish on Adobe as we expected its AI business momentum to reinvigorate growth in its Digital Media business," said Oppenheimer analysts, led by Brian Schwartz, in a detailed investor report on Tuesday. "But this did not play out as we expected, and is visible with Digital Media growth decelerating further in FY25."

Adobe shares were down 2.6% during early market action on Tuesday.

"In our view, Adobe has good medium-term opportunities and is a cheap stock," Schwartz said. "However, a challenging operating environment during the AI technology transition leading to uninspiring and decelerating top-line growth, inconsistent execution with product cycles, durability concerns about the moat, lackluster investor interest for owning software names, and down y/y operating margin guidance in FY26 will likely weigh negatively on the sentiment for the company's opportunities this year, and limit near-term upside for ADBE shares."

This is the latest downgrade to hit Adobe. Last week, BMO and Jefferies downgraded Adobe to Market Perform and Hold, respectively. This followed KeyBanc downgrading the stock to Underweight in mid-December.

It also reflects the broader trend of software names not experiencing the same boost as hardware names during the AI boom.

However, Oppenheimer has a few favorites when it comes to software, including Microsoft (MSFT), Salesforce (CRM), ServiceNow (NOW) and Agilysys (AGYS).

"We think MSFT's Azure results may look better in the 2H:CY26 when new data centers scale," Schwartz said. "NOW and CRM are a thematic play on the AI markets—companies needing to turn things around with investor sentiment (i.e., show-me stocks), and execute well on M&A. Lastly, AGYS should see reacceleration and material profit margin improvement in FY27 on the back of Marriott (MAR)."

data.title: St. Louis Fed's Musalem sees 'little reason' for further rate cuts
data.description:
St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem sees "little reason for further near-term easing," as he considers the Federal Reserve's current policy rate at "right around neutral," meaning the rate is neither fueling nor hindering the U.S. economy, he said on Tuesday.

Policy is in a good place to react in either direction, he added in a webcast sponsored by MNI. Excluding inflation, he estimates that the real policy rate is around 1%. It's "inadvisable" to put policy into an "accommodative stance, at this time," he noted.

There are some downside risks to the labor market and upside risks to inflation, he said. Still, Musalem expects the labor market to stabilize at current levels and inflation to progress toward the Fed's 2% goal.

For the year, he expects the U.S. economy to grow at or above potential this year, he said.

Musalem backed the Federal Reserve's December rate cut, citing some St. Louis Fed research that showed a lower proportion of firms have plans to hire this year and a higher proportion of firms have intentions to reduce their workforce.

There would be a reason to reduce the policy rate if the labor market weakens more than he expects and inflation declines below 2%. "That would allow policy to be eased, without policy going into accommodative territory," he added.

He's "hopeful" that the U.S. is in a higher productivity regime, but it's too early to tell, Musalem noted

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Maersk sends second ship through Red Sea in test of safety
data.description:
A.P. Moller-Maersk (AMKBY) (AMKBF) said Monday it has completed a second transit through the Red Sea, weeks after it tested the route as the ceasefire in Gaza raised hopes for normal shipping traffic.

The company said the Maersk Denver, a U.S.-flagged container vessel, successfully transited the Bab el-Mandeb Strait and into the Red Sea during January 11-12, after its Maersk Sebarok vessel navigated the route last month for the first time in nearly two years.

"Assuming that security thresholds continue to be met, we will continue our stepwise approach towards gradually resuming navigation along the East-West corridor via the Suez Canal and the Red Sea," Maersk (AMKBY) (AMKBF) said, adding it has no additional sailings to announce at this time.

Shipping companies are considering a return to the critical Asia-Europe trade corridor more than two years after they started rerouting vessels around Africa's Cape of Good Hope following attacks on ships in the Red Sea by Houthi rebels.

However, Maersk (AMKBY) (AMKBF) and other container lines have seen improved profit margins from the extra transport time needed to sail south of Africa, which effectively has reduced global vessel capacity by 7%-8% at a time of high competition among shipowners for cargo.

data.title: Ferrari is downgraded at HSBC with catalysts seen only well down the road
data.description:
HSBC lowered its rating on Ferrari (RACE) to Hold from Buy on Tuesday as part of its sweeping look at the European auto sector.

Analyst Michael Tyndall and his team see Ferrari's (RACE) F80 ramp-up and increasing share of specials as key profit drivers that are mostly offset by increased F1 R&D spending and FX headwinds. It was also noted that Ferrari (RACE) management has been clear that any clear margin improvement will be slow and steady. 

"We don’t think the modest growth outlook reflects a weakness in the underlying fundamentals of the business, but rather management’s desire to temper expectations and emphasize the importance of exclusivity, wrote Tyndall. While the firm sees scope for positive surprises concerning the long-term guidance, those catalysts are not expected to materialize in the first half of 2026.

Ferrari has a consensus Buy rating on the books with both Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on RACE is Hold due to a low factor grade for valuation.

Shares of Ferrari (RACE) are down 12.4% over the last 52 weeks.

data.title: Wells Fargo cuts rating of cable operators amid competition from fiber and fixed wireless
data.description:
Wells Fargo Securities analysts issued a stark warning for cable operators on January 13, 2026, downgrading all four major cable stocks including Charter Communications (CHTR), Comcast (CMCSA), Optimum (OPTU), and Cable One (CABO) to Underweight.

The analysts forecast cable companies losing 1 million residential broadband subscribers in 2026 amid fierce competition from fixed wireless access and fiber, which together will claim 100% of net broadband adds.

"We rate all Cable stocks Underweight... we downgrade CHTR to Underweight – new $180 PT," the analysts stated, citing elevated estimate risks from accelerating penetration declines.

In the broadband sector, fixed wireless access and fiber are projected to dominate growth, with fixed wireless access adding nearly 4M subscribers in 2025 and maintaining around 3.7M net adds in 2026 across T-Mobile, Verizon, and AT&T, per the Wells Fargo report. 

Wireless carriers face subscriber deceleration, with postpaid phone net adds dropping 10% for major mobile network operators in 2026 to around 4% industry-wide including cable, due to saturation, immigration headwinds, and fewer price hikes. 

Top picks remain TMUS and T, with TMUS highlighted for 7% organic EBITDA growth in 2026 and nearly 20% FCF surge in 2027, underpinned by better-than-feared subscriber adds and a service revenue premium.

data.title: FDA asks Lilly, Novo to remove suicide warning related to GLP-1 obesity drugs
data.description:
The U.S. FDA on Tuesday requested Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (LLY) to implement labeling changes for their blockbuster weight loss drugs to reflect that there is no increased risk of suicidal behavior or ideation linked to the GLP-1 drugs.

Specifically, the regulator has asked the companies to remove warnings about the potential suicidal behavior or ideation linked to Novo’s (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide), Saxenda (liraglutide), and Lilly’s (LLY) Zepbound (tirzepatide).

The FDA noted that an analysis comprising data from more than 90 placebo-controlled medication trials didn't link GLP-1s with an increased risk for suicidal behavior/ideation or other adverse psychiatric events such as anxiety.

Additionally, the versions of the above drugs approved for diabetes no longer carry warnings about such a risk, the agency said, adding “today’s FDA action will ensure consistent messaging across the labeling for all FDA-approved GLP-1 medications.”

data.title: Revvity expects to report Q4 revenue of $772 million
data.description:
• Revvity (RVTY) on Tuesday said that it expects to post fourth quarter revenue of about $772 million (vs consensus of $759.24M) and full year revenue of ~$2.86 billion (vs consensus of 2.84B).
  • It has projected reported and organic Y/Y revenue growth of  around  6% and 4%, respectively, for the fourth quarter. For the full year 2025, reported and organic revenue growth is expected to be around 4% and 3%, respectively, 
  • The company also now expects its full year adjusted earnings per share to exceed the upper-end of the $4.90-$5.00 guidance (vs consensus of $4.91) provided on October 27, 2025.
  • RVTY is +8.70% to $112.93.
  • Source: Press Release
